process.note: Fresh-style (cook-and-eat) adaptation of Cantonese lap cheong: sweet-salty pork, stuffed and cooked immediately (steam/pan/grill), not air-dried. Cook ground pork to 71°C.

flag: Traditional lap cheong is nitrite-cured AND air-dried ~12 days at ~28-30 g/kg salt — that authentic dried version needs Cure #1/#2. The 'Fresh-style' cook-and-eat item here is correctly uncured.
rationale: Salt 16 g/kg in range; kept cure null because item is explicitly the fresh cook-immediately version, but noted the traditional dried lap cheong is cured.

process.note: Hamonado = sweet fresh Filipino sausage (vs sour/fermented derecado). Fresh cook-and-eat: simmer in a little water then pan-fry/grill. Cook pork to 71°C.

rationale: Hamonado is the sweet fresh (not fermented) longganisa; salt 16 g/kg in range; some recipes add optional pink salt for color but cure not required for a fresh item.

process.note: Horiatiko = country/village style fresh Greek pork sausage with orange peel, garlic, fennel/coriander. Grill fresh to 71°C.

rationale: Village fresh sausage; salt 18 g/kg within range; cure null (aged/smoked loukaniko variants exist but horiatiko is the fresh table sausage).

process.note: Lebanese fresh lamb/beef sausage with warm spices, pine nuts and vinegar/wine; pan-fried or grilled. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh cook-and-eat sausage (vinegar aids preservation, not nitrite cure); salt 18 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: Argentine blood sausage: the blood/fat/onion filling is fully cooked when made, then reheated/charred on the asado ~15 min. Reheat/cook through to 71°C.

rationale: Blood sausage is cooked, not nitrite-cured; salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: Small fresh pork bratwurst with marjoram; Marianski formula ~18 g/kg salt, no cure. Grill/pan fresh to 71°C.

rationale: Classic fresh bratwurst; salt 18 g/kg matches Marianski; cure null.

process.note: Thin coiled fresh Argentine pork/beef grilling sausage; cooked whole over moderate coals until colored and cooked through. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh asado grilling sausage; salt 18 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.ref: Two Guys & a Cooler / Wikipedia — Sai Krok Isan
process.note: Northeastern Thai pork-and-rice sausage traditionally lacto-fermented 2-3 days at ambient temp for its sour tang, then grilled. If made as a fresh cook-immediately item, grill to 71°C.

flag: Traditionally ambient-temp FERMENTED 2-3 days — if produced that way it should use Cure #2 (nitrate) and higher salt (~25-28 g/kg) for botulism safety during the ferment. Only stays uncured if made as a quick fresh sausage grilled the same day. Current salt 14 g/kg is fine for fresh but too low for a real ferment.
rationale: Kept salt/cure as fresh (14 g/kg OK, cure null) but flagged that the authentic fermented version needs Cure #2 and more salt.

process.ref: Hot Thai Kitchen / The Meatwave — Sai Ua
process.note: Northern Thai fresh pork sausage with lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime, curry paste; grilled immediately (optional 1-2 day fridge rest for flavor, not a cure). Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh grill sausage (any fridge rest is refrigerated, not an ambient ferment); salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: Italian fresh pork sausage (fennel/pepper); ~18 g/kg salt, no cure. Grill/pan fresh to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh Italian sausage; salt 18 g/kg in range; cure null (dry-cured salsiccia secca is a separate aged product).

process.note: Cypriot fresh ground pork/lamb wrapped in caul fat (no casing), grilled over coals. Cook through to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh caul-wrapped grill item; salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: 'Taze' = fresh: the beef/lamb sucuk spice mix (cumin, garlic, sumac/red pepper) made WITHOUT Cure #2 and grilled/pan-fried directly like a bratwurst. Cook to 71°C.

flag: Traditional (fermente) sucuk is dry-FERMENTED and air-dried ~1 month and MUST use Cure #2 (nitrate). This item is explicitly the 'taze' (fresh, uncured, cook-and-eat) version, so cure=null is correct — but if the catalog ever means the aged/fermented sucuk it needs Cure #2 and higher salt.
rationale: Confirmed taze = fresh uncured variant (cure null, salt 18 g/kg in range); flagged that the traditional fermented sucuk requires Cure #2.

process.note: Korean blood sausage (pig blood, glass noodles, rice/veg in intestine) is STEAMED fully when made, then reheated/pan-fried. Reheat/cook through to 71°C.

rationale: Steamed blood sausage, cooked not cured; salt 14 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: EU-protected fresh Thuringian pork bratwurst (marjoram/caraway); ~15-18 g/kg salt, no cure. Grill fresh to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh grilling bratwurst; salt 18 g/kg within range (sources cite 15-18); cure null.

process.note: Fresh ground beef/lamb with onion, parsley, warm spices; skewered and grilled ~7-8 min. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh ground-meat grill item; salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.ref: Wikipedia / All We Eat — Lula (lyulya) Kebab
process.note: Fresh minced lamb/beef kneaded and molded onto flat skewers, grilled over coals. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh ground-meat kebab; salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.note: Romanian skinless grilled ground-meat rolls (beef/lamb/pork) with garlic and bicarbonate for spring; grilled fresh. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh skinless grill item (bicarbonate is a texturizer, not a cure); salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.

process.ref: Wikipedia / Where Is My Spoon — Ćevapi
process.note: Balkan skinless grilled minced-meat fingers (beef, often with lamb/pork), garlic and paprika; grilled fresh. Cook to 71°C.

rationale: Fresh skinless grill item; salt 16 g/kg in range; cure null.
